answer:
**Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana** (anonymous), *Chapter 1* (the only chapter in this work).[^1]

The ascetic Kuñjarakarṇa, sent by the Buddha on a tour of hell, arrives at Yama's palace and sees a "great copper cauldron with many spouts... in its courtyard."[^2] He asks Yama about it being scrubbed clean while empty:

> \"Lord Yama, may your glory be eternal.  
> Your compassion for this Kuñjara is fruitful.  
> But what is that cauldron that is being washed?  
> What is its purpose, now that it is clean?\"[^3]  
>   
> \"I am puzzled by it, for it is to be filled,  
> yet it is turned upside down, with nothing inside.  
> What is the reason for it being so empty?  
> This is my question; please tell me.\"[^4]

Yama replies that a great sinner (the gandharva king Pūrṇavijaya) is due soon, with his term in the cauldron lasting a hundred thousand years:

> \"There is a great sinner who is to be cast in.  
> His time in torment will be long.  
> For a hundred thousand years is his term;  
> when it is full, he will be in the cauldron of misery.\"[^5]  
>   
> \"But as for his arrival in Yama's realm,  
> in seven nights he is expected to come.  
> That is the reason the cauldron is being washed,  
> as the place of his torment when he arrives.\"[^6]

Yama adds that afterward, weapon-birds will carry the sinner to an iron tree (to be dashed on its sword-blades), then he will be hung head-down from a bamboo pole over ceaseless fire on the iron plain.[^7]

[^1]: Full work metadata from *get_metadata* confirms this as the sole chapter.
[^2]: [/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-693983](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-693983)
[^3]: [/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-c32761](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-c32761)
[^4]: [/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-2efb11](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-2efb11)
[^5]: [/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-f2b796](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-f2b796)
[^6]: [/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-c757ea](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-c757ea)
[^7]: [/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-6729cc](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-6729cc); [/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-890b15](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-890b15)
